mobility service providers, robotaxis, tier-1 suppliers, and start-ups. Our AV solution also includes the GPU-based hardware required to train the neural networks before their in-vehicle deployment, as well as to re-simulate their operation prior to any over-the-air software updates. We believe our comprehensive, top-to-bottom and end-to-end approach will enable the transportation industry to solve the complex problems arising from the shift to autonomous driving.
7
Table of Contents
Leveraging our intellectual property, or IP.
We believe our IP is a valuable asset that can be accessed by our customers and partners through license and development agreements when they desire to build such capabilities directly into their own products or have us do so through a custom development. Such license and development arrangements can further enhance the reach of our technology.
Sales and Marketing
Our worldwide sales and marketing strategy is key to achieving our objective of providing markets with our high-performance and efficient computing platforms and software. Our sales and marketing teams, located across our global markets, work closely with customers and various industry ecosystems through our partner network. Our partner network incorporates global, regional and specialized CSPs, OEMs, ODMs, ISVs, global system integrators, add-in board manufacturers, or AIBs, distributors, automotive manufacturers and tier-1 automotive suppliers, and other ecosystem participants.
Members of our sales team have technical expertise and product and industry knowledge. We also employ a team of application engineers and solution architects to provide pre-sales assistance to our partner network in designing, testing, and qualifying system designs that incorporate our platforms. For example, our solution architects work with CSPs to provide pre-sales assistance to enable our customers to optimize their hardware and software infrastructure for generative and agentic AI and LLM training and deployment. They also work with foundation model and enterprise software developers to enable our customers to optimize the training and fine-tuning of their models and services, and with enterprise end-users, often in collaboration with their global system integrator of choice, to fine-tune models and build AI applications. We believe that the depth and quality of our design support are key to improving our partner network's time-to-market, maintaining a high level of customer satisfaction, and fostering relationships that encourage our customers and partner network to use the next generation of our products within each platform.
To encourage the development of applications optimized for our platforms and software, we seek to establish and maintain strong relationships in the software development community. Engineering and marketing personnel engage with key software developers to promote and discuss our platforms, as well as to ascertain individual product requirements and solve technical problems. Our developer program supports the development of AI frameworks, SDKs, and APIs for software applications and game titles that are optimized for our platforms. Our Deep Learning Institute provides in-person and online training for developers in industries and organizations around the world to build AI and accelerated computing applications that leverage our platforms.
Seasonality
Our computing platforms serve a diverse set of markets such as data centers, gaming, professional visualization, and automotive. Our desktop gaming products typically see stronger revenue in the second half of our fiscal year. Historical seasonality trends may not repeat.
Manufacturing
We utilize a fabless and contracting manufacturing strategy, whereby we employ and partner with key suppliers for all phases of the manufacturing process, including wafer fabrication, assembly, testing, and packaging. We use the expertise of industry-leading suppliers that are certified by the International Organization for Standardization in such areas as fabrication, assembly, quality control and assurance, reliability, and testing. Additionally, we can avoid many of the significant costs and risks associated with owning and operating manufacturing operations. While we may directly procure certain raw materials used in the production of our products, such as memory, substrates, and a variety of components, our suppliers are responsible for procurement of most raw materials used in the production of our products. As a result, we can focus our resources on product design, quality assurance, marketing, and customer support. In periods of growth, we may place non-cancellable inventory orders for certain product components in advance of our historical lead times, pay premiums, or provide deposits to secure future supply and capacity and may need to continue to do so.
We have expanded our supplier relationships to build redundancy and resilience in our operations to provide long-term manufacturing capacity aligned with growing customer demand. While currently our supply chain is mainly concentrated in Asia, we are expanding into the U.S. and Latin America. We utilize foundries, such as Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited, or TSMC, and Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd., or Samsung, to produce our semiconductor wafers. We purchase memory from SK Hynix Inc., Micron Technology, Inc., and Samsung. We utilize CoWoS technology for semiconductor packaging. We engage with independent subcontractors and contract manufacturers such as Hon Hai Precision Industry Co., Ltd., Wistron Corporation, and Fabrinet to perform assembly, testing and packaging of our final products.
Competition
The market for our products is intensely competitive and is characterized by rapid technological change and evolving industry standards. We believe that the principal competitive factors in this market are performance, breadth of product offerings, access to customers and partners and distribution channels, software support, conformity to industry standard APIs, manufacturing capabilities, processor pricing, and total system costs. We believe that our ability to remain competitive will depend on how well we are able to anticipate the features and functions that customers and partners will
8
Table of Contents
demand and whether we are able to deliver consistent volumes of our products at acceptable levels of quality and at competitive prices. We expect competition to increase from both existing competitors and new market entrants with products that may be lower priced than ours or may provide better performance or additional features not provided by our products. In addition, it is possible that new competitors or alliances among competitors could emerge and acquire significant market share.
A significant source of competition comes from companies that provide or intend to provide GPUs, CPUs, DPUs, embedded SoCs, and other accelerated, AI computing processor products, and providers of semiconductor-based high-performance interconnect products based on InfiniBand, Ethernet, Fibre Channel, and proprietary technologies. Some of our competitors may have greater marketing, financial, distribution and manufacturing resources than we do and may be more able to adapt to customers or technological changes. We expect an increasingly competitive environment in the future.
Our current competitors include:
- suppliers and licensors of hardware and software for discrete and integrated GPUs, custom chips and other accelerated computing solutions, including solutions offered for AI, such as Advanced Micro Devices, Inc., or AMD, Huawei Technologies Co. Ltd., or Huawei, and Intel Corporation, or Intel;

NVDA.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Equity Report: NVDA**  
**Decision Date: 2026-04-01**

### 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory
- **Revenue & Earnings**: Steep quarterly acceleration. Q4 2026 revenue = $68.13B, +19.5% QoQ (vs. Q3 $57.01B). EPS diluted = $1.76, +35.4% QoQ. TTM revenue = $215.94B, TTM net income = $120.07B.
- **Margins**: Exceptional and expanding. Q4 gross margin = 75.0% ($51.09B/$68.13B), operating margin = 65.0% ($44.30B/$68.13B), net margin = 63.1% ($42.96B/$68.13B). TTM operating income = $130.39B.
- **Cash-Flow**: **Not available** for any period in the supplied data.
- **Balance Sheet**: Rapid equity growth. Total equity = $157.29B, up from $49.14B in Q1 2024. Total assets = $206.80B. Total debt = $8.47B, stable over the period.

### 2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience
- **Earnings Quality**: High implied quality from sustained, industry-leading margins (net margin range: 0.5 to 0.6 per valuation data). ROE remains very high at 0.763, though down from 0.919 in mid-2025. The absence of cash-flow data prevents direct assessment of earnings-to-cash conversion.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Minimal leverage and strong solvency. Debt-to-equity = 0.054. Total liabilities ($49.51B) are well-covered by assets, with total equity comprising 76% of total assets.

### 3. Valuation Versus Supplied History
- **Current Multiples** (as of 2026-03-31): PE diluted = 35.59, P/B = 27.18, P/S = 19.80.
- **Historical Context**: The PE diluted has compressed significantly from a peak of 58.715 (August 2025) and now sits near the low end of its one-year range (32.1 to 58.7). P/B is also near the low end of its range (25.7 to 53.8). This compression has occurred despite net margins expanding back to 0.556 and remaining within their range (0.5 to 0.6).

### 4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context
- **Price Level**: Close at $174.40 on 2026-03-31. The 52-week high is $212.19, low is $86.62.
- **Returns vs. Fundamentals**: Quarterly price delta = -7.3% (Q1 2026). This contrasts with the strong fundamental acceleration in the latest quarter (revenue +19.5% QoQ, EPS +35.4% QoQ). The annual price delta remains robust at +59.0%, reflecting the strong prior fundamental trajectory.

### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside
- **Upside**: 1) **Valuation re-rating potential**: Current PE of 35.59 is at the bottom of its one-year range (low: 32.1). Sustained high growth and profitability could justify a multiple expansion toward historical averages. 2) **Exceptional profitability**: Operating margin of 65.0% and ROE of 76.3% provide significant financial leverage for earnings growth.
- **Downside**: 1) **Growth deceleration risk**: The valuation compression from PE >50 to ~35 suggests the market may be discounting a future growth slowdown. 2) **Dependence on continued hyper-growth**: A decline in revenue growth trajectory could lead to further multiple contraction.

### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion
**Fundamentals are strong**: NVDA exhibits explosive revenue growth, elite and expanding profitability margins, and a fortress balance sheet with minimal debt. The recent quarterly price pullback (-7.3%) contrasts with an acceleration in fundamental momentum, causing valuation multiples (PE, P/B) to contract to the lower end of their recent historical ranges.

**Key quantitative trade-off**: The risk is that the market has already priced in a peak growth rate, as evidenced by multiple compression. The reward is that current valuations are near cycle lows despite the company posting record margins and revenues. The conclusion is **provisionally favorable**, contingent on the expectation that the supplied growth and margin trajectory can be sustained. The primary quantitative risk is a future deceleration not yet captured in the provided data, which could pressure multiples further.
NVDA.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: NVDA (NVIDIA)**
**Decision Date:** 2026-04-01

### 1. Business/Franchise Read from Narrative
Based on its 2026-02-25 10-K filing, NVIDIA is positioned as a **full-stack, data center-scale AI infrastructure company**. Its competitive moat stems from a **unified platform strategy** combining accelerated computing hardware (GPUs, CPUs, networking), a deeply embedded **software ecosystem** anchored by CUDA, and a broad library of domain-specific frameworks and models. The company serves four major markets: **Data Center** (for AI training/inference, HPC), **Gaming** (GeForce RTX), **Professional Visualization**, and **Automotive** (DRIVE platform). The narrative emphasizes continuous architectural innovation (e.g., Blackwell, Rubin) to maintain performance leadership, leveraging a "fabless" manufacturing model with key suppliers like TSMC.

### 2. Recent Developments (Dated)
*   **2026-03-31 (Order Backlog):** CEO Jensen Huang disclosed the company has secured a **$1 trillion order book through 2027**, doubling from $500 billion months prior, providing multi-year revenue and margin visibility.
*   **2026-03-31 (Partnership/Investment):** NVIDIA announced a **$2 billion strategic investment in Marvell Technology** and a partnership to collaborate on AI infrastructure, custom processors, and networking technology.
*   **2026-03-31 (Market Technicals):** A general market rally occurred, but NVDA stock was noted to be trading **1.42% below its 20-day SMA** on 2026-03-27, with the Nasdaq in correction territory for the month.
*   **2026-02-25 (Product Cadence):** The 10-K notes the company **accelerated the release cadence of open AI model platforms** like Nemotron and Cosmos to support market development.

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks:**
*   **Legal/Regulatory (Confirmed):** The company faces a **class-action lawsuit** and SEC scrutiny over historical (2017-2018) representation of crypto-mining revenue in its gaming segment, with a $5.5 million SEC sanction already imposed (2026-03-30). Broader regulatory scrutiny on AI and intellectual property is expected as NVIDIA's influence grows (2026-03-13).
*   **Competition (Confirmed/Inferred):** The 10-K explicitly lists competition from **hyperscaler custom silicon** (Alibaba, Alphabet, Amazon) and traditional chipmakers (AMD, Intel). The massive investment and partnership with Marvell (2026-03-31) could be interpreted as both a strengthening of the ecosystem and an admission of the need to counter potential rival custom ASIC efforts.
*   **Sentiment/Valuation Pressure (Inferred):** Prominent investors (Michael Burry, Eric Jackson) publicly indicated **skepticism toward high-flying AI stocks**, with Burry hinting his short positions against NVIDIA may be profitable (2026-03-31). This reflects market risk of sentiment shifts.

**Offsets:**
*   **Demand Visibility:** The disclosed **$1 trillion order backlog** (2026-03-31) provides exceptional forward revenue visibility and suggests strong customer commitement despite competitive threats.
*   **Ecosystem Expansion:** The strategic investment and partnership with Marvell (2026-03-31) deepens ties across the AI infrastructure stack, potentially widening the competitive moat.
*   **Analyst/Platform Support:** The stock received a "BUY" rating from a platform (82.6/100) on 2026-02-25, and bullish technical forecasts were noted on 2026-02-28.

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Positive.** The core franchise as the **dominant platform for AI infrastructure** is robust, underscored by a record **$1 trillion order backlog** providing multi-year visibility. Recent ecosystem scaling via the Marvell partnership reinforces this position. However, the narrative is tempered by **confirmed legal/regulatory overhangs**, **intensifying competition** from hyperscalers, and **volatile market sentiment** toward the AI sector. The balance of evidence at this point-in-time favors continued strong operational momentum, but risks have materially increased.

corrections.issue: The quant report states 'ROE remains very high at 0.763, though down from 0.919 in mid-2025.' The raw valuation data shows ROE=0.919 on 2025-04-01 and 2025-05-05, and ROE=0.916 on 2025-06-05 through 2025-08-12. The decline to 0.865 occurred by 2025-09-15, and further to 0.763 by 2026-02-26. The phrase 'down from 0.919 in mid-2025' is directionally correct but slightly imprecise — the 0.919 reading was in April-May 2025, not mid-2025 (where it was 0.916). Minor issue, no action needed.
corrections.required_action: No action required; minor imprecision in date attribution.

corrections.issue: The quant report states 'The PE diluted has compressed significantly from a peak of 58.715 (August 2025) and now sits near the low end of its one-year range (32.1 to 58.7).' The raw data confirms PE range 32.1..58.7 and the peak at 58.715 on 2025-08-12. However, the report also says 'P/B is also near the low end of its range (25.7 to 53.8).' The latest P/B is 27.18, which is indeed near the low of 25.7. This is consistent with the raw data. No issue.
corrections.required_action: No action required; claim is supported.

This is consistent. No issue.
corrections.required_action: No action required; claim is supported by raw evidence.

corrections.issue: The qual report states 'The company faces a class-action lawsuit and SEC scrutiny over historical (2017-2018) representation of crypto-mining revenue in its gaming segment, with a $5.5 million SEC sanction already imposed (2026-03-30).' The raw evidence confirms the $5.5 million SEC sanction and the class-action lawsuit from the news articles dated 2026-03-27 and 2026-03-30. This is consistent. No issue.
corrections.required_action: No action required; claim is supported.

corrections.issue: The quant report notes 'Cash-Flow: Not available for any period in the supplied data' and 'The absence of cash-flow data prevents direct assessment of earnings-to-cash conversion.' The raw data confirms all cash flow entries show '(no data)'. This is correctly flagged. However, the quant report's conclusion of 'provisionally favorable' should note that the inability to verify cash conversion is a material gap given the company's scale ($120B TTM net income). The report does mention this as a limitation but does not weight it heavily in the conclusion.
corrections.required_action: PM should note that the absence of cash flow data is a material gap in assessing earnings quality at this scale; the favorable conclusion is conditional on this data gap.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: NVIDIA's fundamentals are accelerating (Q4 revenue $68.13B, +19.5% QoQ; net margin 63.1%) while valuation has compressed to the low end of its one-year P/E range (35.6x vs. 32.1-58.7x), creating a favorable risk-reward setup. The $1 trillion order backlog through 2027 provides exceptional forward visibility that substantially de-risks the growth trajectory, though the absence of cash flow data and rising competitive/regulatory headwinds temper conviction.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: NVIDIA's fundamentals are accelerating (Q4 revenue $68.13B, +19.5% QoQ; net margin 63.1%) while valuation has compressed to the low end of its one-year P/E range (35.6x vs. 32.1-58.7x), creating a favorable risk-reward setup. The $1 trillion order backlog through 2027 provides exceptional forward visibility that substantially de-risks the growth trajectory, though the absence of cash flow data and rising competitive/regulatory headwinds temper conviction.
key_factors: Valuation re-rating potential: P/E of 35.59 sits at the bottom of its one-year range (32.1-58.7) despite Q4 revenue accelerating to $68.13B (+19.5% QoQ) and net margins expanding to 63.1%, suggesting the market has over-discounted growth deceleration.

$1 trillion order backlog through 2027 (disclosed 2026-03-31 by CEO Jensen Huang, doubling from $500B months prior) provides multi-year revenue visibility that is rare for any company and substantially de-risks forward earnings.

Exceptional profitability metrics: operating margin of 65.0%, ROE of 76.3%, and TTM net income of $120.07B on a fortress balance sheet (D/E of 0.054, equity of $157.29B) provide significant financial leverage.

Rising competitive and regulatory head:: legal overhang (SEC $5.5M sanction, class-action lawsuit) and hyperscaler custom silicon competition create material headwinds that limit upside and justify a below-peak multiple.

Investments in Privately Held Companies
We make investments in privately held companies that develop technology or provide services that are complementary to our products or that provide insights into emerging technologies that may become relevant to our businesses. Strategic Alliances
We pursue strategic alliances with other companies in areas where collaboration can produce industry advancement and accelerate new markets. The objectives and goals of a strategic alliance can include one or more of the following: technology exchange, product development, joint sales and marketing, or new market creation.

Competition
We compete in the networking and communications equipment markets, providing products and services designed to transport, and help secure data, voice, and video traffic across cloud, private and public networks and the Internet. We compete with numerous vendors in each product category. The overall number of our competitors providing niche product solutions may increase. Also, the identity and composition of competitors may change as we increase our activity in newer product areas, and in key priority areas. As we continue to expand globally, we may see new competition in different geographic regions. In particular, we have experienced price-focused competition from competitors in Asia, especially from China, and we anticipate this will continue.

Some of our competitors compete across many of our product lines, while others are primarily focused in a specific product area. In addition, some of our competitors may have greater resources, including technical and engineering resources, than we do. As we expand into new markets, we will face competition not only from our existing competitors but also from other competitors, including existing companies with strong technological, marketing, and sales positions in those markets. We also sometimes face competition from resellers and distributors of our products. Companies with which we have strategic alliances in some areas may be competitors in other areas, and this trend may increase. For example, the enterprise data center is undergoing a fundamental transformation arising from the convergence of technologies, including computing, networking, storage, and software, that previously were segregated. We also face competition from customers to which we license or supply technology and suppliers from which we transfer technology. The inherent nature of networking requires interoperability. As such, we must cooperate and, at the same time, compete with many companies. ### Quantitative Equity Report: CSCO (as of 2026-04-01)

#### 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory
- **Revenue**: Increased consecutively from $12.70B (Q3 2024) to $15.35B (Q2 2026). Latest QoQ growth: +3.1%; YoY growth: +9.7%. TTM revenue: $59.05B.
- **Earnings**: EPS diluted rose from $0.46 (Q3 2024) to $0.80 (Q2 2026). Latest QoQ EPS growth: +11.1%; YoY growth: +31.1%. TTM net income: $11.08B.
- **Margins**: Gross margin: 0.648 (latest valuation), with quarterly gross profit increasing from $8.27B to $9.97B. Operating margin: 0.227. Net margin improved from approximately 0.149 (Q3 2024) to 0.206 (Q2 2026), with latest valuation net margin at 0.188.
- **Cash-Flow**: Data unavailable for all quarters (explicitly marked).
- **Balance Sheet**: Total assets ranged from $119.78B (Q3 2025) to $124.41B (Q4 2024). Total equity increased from $45.77B (Q3 2024) to $47.72B (Q2 2026). Total debt rose from $20.10B (Q3 2024) to $24.60B (Q2 2026). Debt-to-equity ratio: 0.515.

#### 2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience
- **Earnings Quality**: Cash flow data unavailable, preventing direct assessment of cash conversion. However, EPS growth outpaced revenue growth (YoY EPS +31.1% vs. revenue +9.7%), indicating improving profitability, but durability is unconfirmed without cash flow.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Debt-to-equity ratio of 0.515 is moderate. Equity showed steady growth (from $45.77B to $47.72B over eight quarters), and total assets consistently exceeded liabilities (e.g., $123.37B assets vs. $75.65B liabilities in Q2 2026), suggesting resilience.

#### 3. Valuation versus Company's Own Supplied History
- **Latest Valuation**: pe_diluted=27.909, p_b=6.477, p_s=5.235, earnings_yield=0.036 as of 2026-03-31.
- **Historical Range** (past year): pe_diluted ranged from 23.2 to 33.5; p_b from 4.7 to 7.4. Current values are within these ranges, neither extreme. Net margin improved from 0.17 to 0.188, and ROE increased from 0.202 to 0.232 over the same period. Valuation is moderate relative to its history, with no clear discount or premium based solely on multiple levels.

#### 4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context versus Fundamental Path
- **Price Level**: Latest close $77.59, with a 52-week high of $88.19 and low of $52.11.
- **Quarterly Return** (from 2025-12-29): -0.3%, indicating stagnation.
- **Annual Return** (from 2025-03-28): +27.5%, reflecting strong appreciation.
- **Fundamental Path Comparison**: Revenue and EPS grew steadily over the same period (e.g., Q1 2025 revenue $13.84B, EPS $0.68; Q2 2026 revenue $15.35B, EPS $0.80). The annual price return aligns with this growth, but the recent quarterly flatness contrasts with continued fundamental improvement (Q2 2026 QoQ revenue +3.1%, EPS +11.1%).

#### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside
- **Upside**: Strong earnings growth trajectory (YoY EPS +31.1%) with expanding margins (net margin increased from ~0.17 to ~0.206 over eight quarters). Balance sheet equity growth supports financial stability. If revenue and margin trends persist, earnings could drive further upside.
- **Downside**: Valuation pe_diluted=27.909 may compress if earnings growth decelerates. Debt increased from $20.10B to $24.60B, though manageable. Lack of cash flow data raises uncertainty about earnings quality. Price has stalled after a significant annual run-up (+27.5%), potentially indicating limited near-term momentum without fundamental catalysts.

#### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion
The financial trajectory shows solid revenue and earnings growth with improving margins and a resilient balance sheet, but cash flow data is unavailable to assess earnings quality fully. Valuation is within historical norms, not signaling cheapness or expensiveness. Price has appreciated substantially over the year but is flat recently, with fundamentals still advancing. The quantitative risk lies in valuation multiple compression and unverified cash generation, while the reward depends on continued growth and margin expansion. On balance, the numbers suggest neutral to slightly positive risk/reward, contingent on sustenance of current fundamentals.
CSCO.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Analysis Report: Cisco Systems, Inc. (CSCO)**
**Report Date:** 2026-04-01
**Evidence Through:** 2026-03-31

### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Based on the company's 10-K filing (period ending 2025-07-26), Cisco designs and sells a broad range of networking, security, collaboration, and observability technologies. Its strategy centers on "One Cisco," integrating its portfolio to deliver three key customer outcomes: **AI-ready data centers**, **future-proofed workplaces**, and **digital resilience**. The business is organized globally into three geographic segments (Americas, EMEA, APJC) and sells to enterprises, public institutions, and service providers (including large webscale providers). Core product categories include:
*   **Networking:** Industry-leading switching, routing, wireless, and server hardware/software (including the Catalyst 9000, Nexus 9000, and new AI-optimized Smart Switches).
*   **Security:** A portfolio spanning network security, SASE, and threat detection/response, significantly bolstered by the acquisition of Splunk.
*   **Collaboration:** The Webex suite, contact center, and CPaaS offerings.
*   **Observability:** Solutions including ThousandEyes and the Splunk Observability Suite for end-to-end visibility.

The franchise is characterized by a large installed base, a broad integrated portfolio, and a shift towards more software and subscription-based offerings.

### 2. Recent Narrative Developments (PIT Dated)
*   **AI & Strategic Partnerships (2026-03-17):** Cisco and Nvidia announced an expansion of their **Secure AI Factory** platform to help enterprises deploy and secure AI across data centers and the edge, aiming to reduce deployment timelines from months to weeks. (Source: 2026-03-17 news summary).
*   **Market Sentiment & Sector Momentum (2026-03-24):** Cisco's stock posted gains alongside peers like Nokia and Ericsson, reflecting positive sentiment in the telecom equipment sector driven by **5G infrastructure demand and North American carrier spending trends**. (Source: 2026-03-24 news summary).
*   **Analyst Sentiment (2026-02-20):** A Seeking Alpha article cited Cisco as a "buy," driven by strong demand, robust earnings, and an attractive valuation versus peers, noting Q2 revenue growth of 9.3% YoY. (Source: 2026-02-20 web context).
*   **Cost & Margin Concern (2026-02-12):** Following earnings, some Wall Street commentary expressed concern over **rising memory costs**, which could pressure margins, despite "solid" overall results and guidance. (Source: 2026-02-12 web context).

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks:**
*   **Legal & Regulatory Exposure:** The company faces ongoing legal actions, including a U.S. Supreme Court petition (*Cisco Systems v. Doe I*) related to aiding-and-abetting liability (Source: 2026-02-25 web context) and historical antitrust allegations (Source: 2026-03-28 web context). A 2026-03-27 source also notes Cisco has argued against certain DNS filtering proposals due to engineering costs and incompatibility risks.
*   **Cost Pressures:** Analyst commentary has flagged **rising memory costs** as a potential headwind to gross margins (Source: 2026-02-12).
*   **Competitive Intensity:** The 10-K lists a broad set of competitors across all segments, including hyperscalers (AWS, Microsoft), pure-play security firms (Palo Alto, CrowdStrike), and networking rivals (Arista, Huawei), with low barriers to entry in some areas.
*   **Geopolitical & Macroeconomic Sensitivity:** Business is global and exposed to economic cycles, geopolitical tensions (e.g., noted 2026-03-17 regarding the Strait of Hormuz), and foreign currency fluctuations.

**Offsets:**
*   **Strategic Positioning in Key Growth Markets:** Cisco is directly investing in and partnering for the **AI infrastructure build-out** (e.g., with Nvidia) and benefits from secular trends in **5G, cybersecurity, and IoT** (e.g., the cited growth projections for optical interconnects, LoRaWAN, and Wi-Fi 6 markets).
*   **Portfolio Integration & Scale:** The "One Cisco" strategy and integrated portfolio (enhanced by Splunk) create a differentiated offering for customers seeking simplified, secure, and resilient network architectures, which is a competitive moat.
*   **Diversified Customer Base:** Serving enterprises, public sector, and service providers provides revenue diversification and reduces reliance on any single market vertical.

### 4. Qualitative Assessment
**Assessment: Neutral with Cautious Positive Tilt**

**Rationale:** The evidence points to a company with a strong, strategically evolving franchise that is actively aligning with major secular tech trends (AI, 5G, integrated security). Recent developments, particularly the expanded Nvidia partnership and positive sector momentum, are positive catalysts. However, these are counterbalanced by meaningful ongoing legal headwinds, noted cost pressures, and inherent execution risks in navigating a highly competitive landscape. The stock's mixed reaction to AI expansion news (as of 2026-03-17) suggests the market is awaiting tangible proof of accelerated growth. The qualitative picture is therefore one of stable defensive characteristics with growth optionality, but not without near-term risks that temper a decisively positive stance.

**Evidence vs. Inference:**
*   **Confirmed:** The Nvidia partnership (2026-03-17), sector stock performance (2026-03-24), analyst buy rating and memory cost concern (2026-02-20, 2026-02-12), and specific legal cases cited are all directly from the supplied text.
*   **Inference:** The assessment of "cautious positive tilt" infers potential upside from the company's strategic actions in AI and market tailwinds, but this is not yet confirmed by recent financial results in the provided evidence.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Total debt rose from $20.10B (Q3 2024) to $24.60B (Q2 2026)' implying a steady increase. The raw balance sheet shows debt was flat at $20.10B through Q1 2025, then jumped to $25.10B in Q3 2025, and decreased to $24.60B in Q4 2025 where it remained. The trajectory was not a steady rise but a step-change followed by a slight decline. This is a minor narrative imprecision but does not materially alter the conclusion.
corrections.required_action: No action required; noted for PM awareness that debt increase was a discrete step (likely related to Splunk acquisition financing or similar), not a gradual trend.

corrections.issue: Qual report states 'The stock's mixed reaction to AI expansion news (as of 2026-03-17) suggests the market is awaiting tangible proof of accelerated growth.' The raw evidence confirms Cisco was 'down 0.71% in premarket' on 2026-03-17 despite the Nvidia partnership announcement, and the broader market was also down due to geopolitical tensions (Strait of Hormuz). The 'mixed reaction' characterization is supported but the attribution to market skepticism about AI growth is an inference — the decline could equally reflect the macro/geopolitical sell-off noted in the same source.
corrections.required_action: No action required; the inference is reasonable but PM should note the confounding macro factor.

However, the range notation 'net_margin 0.2..0.2' in the raw data appears to be a display artifact (likely 0.17..0.188 or similar). The quant report correctly uses the actual sparse data points rather than the truncated range, so no error in the report itself.
corrections.required_action: No action required.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Cisco's AI-driven growth cycle remains real and accelerating, with YoY EPS growth of +31.1% and expanding margins, but the stock's valuation (P/E ~28x, P/B ~6.5x) sits in the upper half of its historical range and the recent quarterly price stagnation (-0.3%) suggests the market has already priced in much of the near-term upside. The risk/reward is balanced—solid fundamentals support the current price, but limited margin of safety and unverified cash flow quality cap upside potential.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Cisco's AI-driven growth cycle remains real and accelerating, with YoY EPS growth of +31.1% and expanding margins, but the stock's valuation (P/E ~28x, P/B ~6.5x) sits in the upper half of its historical range and the recent quarterly price stagnation (-0.3%) suggests the market has already priced in much of the near-term upside. The risk/reward is balanced—solid fundamentals support the current price, but limited margin of safety and unverified cash flow quality cap upside potential.
key_factors: Q2 FY2026 results show continued momentum: revenue $15.35B (+9.7% YoY), EPS $0.80 (+31.1% YoY), with net margin expanding from ~0.17 to ~0.206 over eight quarters, confirming the AI networking demand cycle is delivering.

Valuation is moderate-to-stretched: P/E of 27.9x and P/B of 6.5x sit within the upper half of their one-year ranges (P/E 23.2–33.5, P/B 4.7–7.4), leaving limited margin of safety if growth decelerates.

Debt increased from $20.1B to $24.6B (step-change likely tied to Splunk financing), with D/E at 0.515—manageable but higher than pre-acquisition levels, and cash flow data unavailable to confirm earnings quality.

Strategic positioning is supportive: expanded Nvidia Secure AI Factory partnership (2026-03-17) and positive sector momentum in 5G/telecom equipment (2026-03-24) provide growth optionality, though the stock's muted reaction to the Nvidia news suggests the market awaits tangible revenue acceleration from AI initiatives.

HON.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: HON (Honeywell International Inc.)**
**Decision Date: 2026-04-01**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
*   **Revenue:** Shows moderate growth. TTM revenue is $37.73B. Quarterly revenue increased from $9.32B (2025-06-30) to $9.76B (2025-12-31), a QoQ increase of +4.7%.
*   **Earnings:** Deteriorated sharply in the most recent quarter. TTM net income is $4.33B. However, Q4 2025 reported a net loss of -$123.00M, resulting in a diluted EPS of -$0.18. This represents a QoQ EPS decline of -107.3% from the prior Q2's $2.45.
*   **Margins:** Pressured in the latest period. TTM gross margin is 36.9% and operating margin is 21.7%. In Q4 2025, gross profit was $3.47B on revenue of $9.76B, implying a gross margin of ~35.6%. Operating income was negative at -$106.00M.
*   **Cash-Flow:** Not available. No cash flow statements are provided in the supplied data.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Leverage has increased while equity has eroded. Total debt rose from $23.44B (2024-03-31) to $29.05B (2025-12-31). Total equity declined from $18.02B (2025-03-31) to $15.03B (2025-12-31). The debt-to-equity ratio stands at 1.933.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** Appears low in the latest quarter. The swing from a profitable Q2 2025 (EPS $2.45) to a loss-making Q4 2025 (EPS -$0.18) on modestly higher revenue indicates significant margin compression or large non-recurring charges, which are not broken out. TTM metrics are positive but mask the Q4 deterioration.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Moderately strained. Total equity of $15.03B against total debt of $29.05B yields a debt-to-equity of 1.933. The trajectory shows debt increasing and equity decreasing over the provided periods.

**3. Valuation vs. Company's Own Supplied History**
*   Current valuation metrics are extreme outliers relative to the recent one-year window.
*   **Price-to-Earnings (Diluted):** The latest reading of 61.421 is at the top end of the 365-day window range of 19.8 to 67.4. It is elevated versus the median range observed from Apr-Oct 2025, which was ~22x-24x.
*   **Price-to-Book:** The latest reading of 19.334 is also at the top end of its window range of 6.3 to 21.2, far above the ~7x-8x levels seen throughout most of 2025.
*   **Net Margin & ROE:** The latest net margin is 0.127 (12.7%), within its window range of 0.1 to 0.2. ROE remains at 0.317 (31.7%), which is stable across the provided history.
*   **Key Observation:** Valuation multiples have expanded dramatically (PE from ~23x to 61x; PB from ~7x to 19x) while the latest quarterly fundamental (net income) has deteriorated.

**4. Price Level and Return Context vs. Fundamental Path**
*   **Price Level:** $452.06 as of 2026-03-31.
*   **Return Context:** Price performance is extremely strong and disconnected from recent earnings momentum. The stock returned +129.4% over the prior quarter (~63 trading days from 2025-12-29) and +128.0% annually (~252 trading days from 2025-03-28).
*   **Path Comparison:** The price nearly tripled over the year while TTM earnings grew modestly. The massive QoQ price surge of +129.4% occurred into a quarter (Q4 2025) that reported a net loss, creating a stark divergence between price trajectory and the fundamental earnings path.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
*   **Upside:** The strongest quantitative upside rests on the premise that the Q4 2025 loss is an anomaly. If earnings revert to the TTM average run-rate (~$1.08B/quarter) or the prior Q2 level ($1.57B), the high forward multiple could be justified by growth expectations. The stable ROE of 0.317 (31.7%) also indicates strong capital efficiency historically.
*   **Downside:** The strongest quantitative downside stems from valuation and balance sheet risk. The PE of 61.421 prices in significant future growth, leaving little margin for error. Combined with a debt-to-equity of 1.933 and an eroding equity base, any sustained weakness in earnings or margins could lead to a severe valuation de-rating. The recent price momentum is unsupported by the latest quarterly earnings data.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
Based strictly on the supplied point-in-time numbers, the risk/reward profile appears unfavorable. The valuation (PE 61.4, PB 19.3) has expanded to historical extremes while the most recent quarterly earnings quality deteriorated (negative EPS of -$0.18). The balance sheet shows rising leverage. The +129% quarterly price surge has occurred despite these fundamental headwinds, creating a significant disconnect. Quantitative risk is elevated given the stretched valuation and volatile earnings; potential reward depends on a reversion to higher historical earnings levels, which is not evidenced in the latest data.
HON.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: Honeywell International Inc. (HON)**
**Decision Date: 2026-04-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Based on the supplied narrative, Honeywell operates as a diversified industrial conglomerate with key activities in aerospace and defense, building technologies, and energy and sustainability solutions. The company is actively reshaping its portfolio, evidenced by its planned spin-off of the aerospace division and the acquisition of a catalyst technologies business to bolster its energy segment. Its franchise is supported by long-term contracts with entities like the U.S. Department of Defense and participation in large-scale government initiatives like the Pentagon's SHIELD program.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **Corporate Action / Spin-Off:** On 2026-03-03, Honeywell filed a Form 10 registration statement with the SEC to spin off its Aerospace division into an independent public company (expected ticker HONA) in Q3 2026. The unit had 2025 pro forma net sales of $17.4 billion. On 2026-03-25, the company priced a $17 billion senior notes offering related to the spin-off.
*   **Acquisition:** On 2026-02-23, Honeywell renegotiated its acquisition of Johnson Matthey's Catalyst Technologies business, reducing the price from $2.43 billion to $1.79 billion. The deal is expected to close by end of August 2026 and will be integrated into the Energy and Sustainability Solutions segment.
*   **Contracts & Partnerships:** On 2026-03-25, Honeywell secured a $500 million contract with the U.S. Department of Defense to upgrade aerospace and defense production capacity. On 2026-03-26, it announced a strategic partnership with Rhombus to deliver integrated cloud-based building security solutions.
*   **Capital Allocation:** On 2026-02-13, the Board declared a quarterly dividend of $1.19 per share.
*   **Market Outlook:** Web context from 2026-03-16 notes management's 2026 outlook sets a "more measured pace," representing a deceleration from the prior year's 12% adjusted EPS growth.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Execution & Disruption Risk (Confirmed):** The planned aerospace spin-off carries risk of "disruption, including disputes, litigation or unanticipated costs" (2026-03-06). The process is a major structural change.
    *   **Litigation & Regulatory Risk (Confirmed):** The company faces various lawsuits, including allegations of employment discrimination (2026-03-27), challenges to federal contractor vaccine mandates (2026-03-09), and securities law inquiries (2026-03-23). These represent ongoing legal and reputational risks.
    *   **Market & Technical Momentum (Confirmed):** As of 2026-03-26, shares were trading below key moving averages with "mixed technical momentum" amid broader market weakness.

corrections.issue: Qual report contains extensive garbled/corrupted text in the Offsets section (e.g., 'widely as as as as by2 the ( ( (2 the...' and foreign-language noise), rendering that section unreliable for the PM.
corrections.required_action: Flag to PM that the qualitative Offsets section is corrupted and should not be relied upon; request a clean re-issuance of that subsection.

corrections.issue: Qual report states 'shares were trading below key moving averages with mixed technical momentum' as of 2026-03-26, but the quant evidence shows the stock at $452.06 on 2026-03-31 after a +129.4% quarterly surge. The qual report's technical characterization appears stale (referencing a single-day dip amid broader market weakness) and could mislead the PM into underweighting the massive price momentum.
corrections.required_action: Note to PM: the 'below key moving averages' language is from a single news item dated 2026-03-26 and does not reflect the broader +129% quarterly price surge documented in the quant pack. Treat the technical risk note as a short-term observation, not a structural bearish signal.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Cash-Flow: Not available' and 'free_cash_flow=n/a' throughout, yet the TTM summary line lists 'free_cash_flow=n/a' alongside other TTM figures. The report correctly flags this gap but does not explicitly warn the PM that the absence of cash-flow data preventses a material blind spot for assessing whether the Q4 loss is cash-destructive or accrual-driven.
corrections.required_action: Advisory to PM: no cash-flow data is available in the supplied packs. The Q4 2025 net loss of -$123M cannot be assessed for cash quality. This is a material evidentiary gap before sizing any position.

unsupported_claims: Qual report claims 'management's 2026 outlook sets a more measured pace, representing a deceleration from the prior year's 12% adjusted EPS growth.' The 12% figure appears only in a web-context summary (bitget.com, 2026-03-16) and is not corroborated by any filing or primary-source data in the raw packs. The claim is sourced but from a third-party commentary, not an official filing.

Qual report states the aerospace unit had '2025 pro forma net sales of $17.4 billion' and '$4.3 billion in adjusted EBIT.' These figures come from the news summary for the 2026-03-03 filing item and are not independently verified against a 10-K or Form 10 in the raw packs (the 10-K business section is listed as 'none'). The numbers are attributed but not cross-checked.

Quant report states 'The latest net margin is 0.127 (12.7%)' and 'ROE remains at 0.317 (31.7%)'. The raw valuation data confirms net_margin=0.127 and roe=0.317 for the latest period, but the ROE figure of 31.7% appears constant across the entire 12-point history (every row shows roe=0.317), which is suspicious and may reflect a data artifact rather than a stable fundamental. The quant report does not flag this anomaly.

Quant report states 'Total debt rose from $23.44B (2024-03-31) to $29.05B (2025-12-31).' The raw balance sheet confirms these figures. However, the report omits that total_debt actually declined from $30.24B (2025-06-30) to $29.05B (2025-12-31), presenting only the most alarming trajectory. This is a selective framing issue rather than an outright error.

Qual report references 'securities law inquiries (2026-03-23)' citing a Glancy Prongay source. The raw evidence shows this is a law firm solicitation for potential plaintiffs, not a confirmed regulatory inquiry or filed lawsuit. The characterization as 'securities law inquiries' overstates the materiality of the evidence provided.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: HON has nearly tripled in price over the past year (+128%) while the most recent quarter reported a net loss (-$0.18 EPS), leverage has ballooned to 1.93x D/E, and valuation has stretched to 61x trailing P/E and 19x P/B—historically extreme levels. The aerospace spin-off and catalyst acquisition are real catalysts, but the price already reflects them and then some, leaving a deeply unfavorable risk/reward.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: HON has nearly tripled in price over the past year (+128%) while the most recent quarter reported a net loss (-$0.18 EPS), leverage has ballooned to 1.93x D/E, and valuation has stretched to 61x trailing P/E and 19x P/B—historically extreme levels. The aerospace spin-off and catalyst acquisition are real catalysts, but the price already reflects them and then some, leaving a deeply unfavorable risk/reward.
key_factors: Valuation at historical extremes: P/E of 61.4 and P/B of 19.3 are at the top of their 365-day ranges (P/E range 19.8–67.4, P/B range 6.3–21.2), having expanded from ~23x and ~7x respectively through most of 2025, while the latest quarterly earnings deteriorated to a net loss.

Q4 2025 earnings collapse: Diluted EPS of -$0.18 (net loss of -$123M) on $9.76B revenue, representing a -107.3% QoQ decline from Q2's $2.45 EPS, with operating income negative at -$106M—indicating severe margin compression or large charges that the TTM figures mask.

Balance sheet deterioration: Total debt increased from $23.4B (Mar 2024) to $29.1B (Dec 2025) while equity eroded from $18.0B to $15.0B, pushing D/E to 1.933—leverage is elevated and rising, limiting financial flexibility during a major corporate restructuring.

Price-fundamental disconnect: The stock surged +129.4% in the latest quarter and +128% annually to $452.06, nearly tripling, while TTM earnings grew only modestly and Q4 reported a loss—this extreme momentum into deteriorating fundamentals creates significant mean-reversion risk.

dustry segment, and range from large multinational enterprises to smaller, more narrowly focused entities. Across our business segments, we recognize hundreds of competitors worldwide and as we execute our hybrid cloud and AI strategy, we are regularly exposed to new competitors.
Our principal methods of competition are: technology innovation; performance; price; quality; brand; our breadth of capabilities, products and services; talent; client relationships and trust; the ability to deliver business value to clients; and service and support. In order to maintain leadership, we optimize our portfolio with organic and inorganic innovations and effective resource allocation. These investments not only drive current performance but will extend our innovation leadership into the future.
Our breadth and depth of expertise enables us to take different technologies and bring them together to solve the most pressing business issues of our clients. We differentiate from other providers by bringing together incumbency with enterprises, deep expertise in technology, industries and business processes, a broad go-to-market reach including an ecosystem of partners and alliances, and a strong focus on innovation. All of these attributes position IBM for accelerated growth now and prepare us for the next set of business opportunities, such as quantum computing.
1
Table of Contents
Overall, the company is the leader or among the leaders in each of our business segments. A summary of the competitive environment for each business segment is included below:
Software:
The depth, breadth, and innovation of our software offerings, coupled with our global reach, deep industry expertise and research capabilities help differentiate our offerings from our competitors. Our hybrid cloud and AI platforms allow clients to realize their digital and AI transformations across the applications, data, and environments in which they operate. The principal competitors in this segment include: Alphabet (Google), Amazon, BMC, Broadcom, Microsoft, Oracle, Salesforce, SAP and Splunk, a CISCO Company. We also compete with smaller, niche competitors in specific geographic regions or product segments.
Consulting:
Consulting integrates strategy, experience design, technology and operations expertise by domain across industries to deliver transformation for clients. Consulting operates in a highly competitive, dynamic market that spans business consulting, systems integration, application development and management, and business process outsourcing services. Our competitors include global firms such as Accenture, Capgemini, India-based service providers, management consulting firms, the consulting practices of public accounting firms, engineering service providers, and niche specialists. Our competitive position is supported by industry expertise; hybrid cloud, data, and AI capabilities; and the use of IBM technology and ecosystem partners to deliver solutions aligned to clients' strategic priorities.
Infrastructure:
IBM is well positioned in the growing hybrid cloud infrastructure market, providing on-premises and cloud-based server and storage solutions. We gain advantage and differentiation through investments in higher-value capabilities, including security, scalability, and reliability, designed especially for mission-critical and AI workloads. In addition, we offer a portfolio of life-cycle services for hybrid cloud infrastructure deployment. Our principal competitors include: Dell Technologies, Hewlett-Packard Enterprise (HPE), Intel, NetApp and Pure Storage as well as original device manufacturers (ODMs) who provide systems that are re-branded. Further, cloud service providers are leveraging innovation in technology and service delivery to compete with traditional providers and to offer additional routes to market for server and storage systems.
Financing:
Financing provides client and commercial financing, facilitating IBM clients' acquisition of hardware, software and services. Financing's ability to manage credit and residual value risk generates a competitive advantage for the company. The key competitive factors include: interest rates charged, IT product experience, client service, contract flexibility, ease of doing business, global capabilities and residual values. In client and commercial financing, Financing primarily competes with non-captive financing entities and financial institutions.
Forward-Looking and Cautionary Statements
Certain statements contained in this Form 10-K may constitute "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995 ("Reform Act"). Forward-looking statements are based on the company's current assumptions regarding future business and financial performance. These statements by their nature address matters that are uncertain to different degrees. The company may also make forward-looking statements in other reports filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC"), in materials delivered to stockholders and in press releases. In addition, the company's representatives may from time to time make oral forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements provide current expectations of future events based on certain assumptions and include any statement that does not directly relate to any historical or current fact. Words such as "anticipates," "believes," "expects," "estimates," "intends," "plans," "projects," and similar expressions, may identify such forward-looking statements. Any forward-looking statement in this Form 10-K speaks only as of the date on which it is made. Except as required by law, the company assumes no obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statements. In accordance with the Reform Act, set forth under Item 1A. "Risk Factors" on pages 3 to 10 are cautionary statements that accompany those forward-looking statements. Readers should carefully review such cautionary statements as they identify certain important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from those in the forward-looking statements and from historical trends. **IBM Quantitative Report**  
*Decision Date: 2026-04-01 | Evidence Cutoff: 2026-03-31*

**1. Financial Trajectory**  
- **Revenue**: Quarterly revenue fluctuates between $14.46B (2024-Q1) and $17.55B (2024-Q4). The latest reported quarter (2025-Q2) is $16.98B. *2025-Q4 data unavailable.*  
- **Earnings**: Quarterly EPS ranges from –$0.36 (2024-Q3) to $3.11 (2024-Q4). Latest EPS (2025-Q2) is $2.31. *2025-Q4 earnings not supplied.*  
- **Margins**: Gross margin fluctuates (53.5%–59.5% from quarterly data). Latest valuation ratios show gross margin 58.2%, operating margin 15.3%, net margin 15.7%. *TTM and growth calculations marked n/a.*  
- **Cash Flow**: *No cash flow data provided for any period.*  
- **Balance Sheet**: Total equity increased from $23.33B (2024-Q1) to $32.74B (2025-Q4). Debt-to-equity (liabilities/equity) improved from 4.88× (2024-Q1) to 3.64× (2025-Q4). Total assets rose to $151.88B.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**  
- **Earnings Quality**: *Incomplete assessment due to missing 2025-Q4 income statement and cash flow data.* Quarterly EPS shows volatility with one negative quarter. Net margin improved from 9.1% (Jan-2026) to 15.7% (Feb-2026) in valuation history, but source data for this shift not fully visible.  
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Equity growth is positive, but leverage remains high (liabilities/equity = 3.64×). Asset growth (from $137.17B to $151.88B) outpaces liability growth (from $113.84B to $119.14B). *No cash flow data to assess liquidity or debt coverage.*

**3. Valuation vs. Own History**  
- **Current multiples** (2026-03-31): P/E 21.7, P/B 7.023, P/S 3.405, earnings yield 4.6%.  
- **Historical range** (Apr 2025–Mar 2026): P/E 20.5–51.2, P/B 6.6–10.8. Current P/E is at the **bottom of its 1-year range**.  
- **Fundamental support**: ROE improved from 22.0% (Apr 2025) to 32.4% (Feb 2026); net margin improved from 9.6% to 15.7% over the same period. *Sustainability of this improvement not verifiable without 2025-Q4 income details.*

**4. Price Level & Returns vs. Fundamentals**  
- **Price**: $242.39 (2026-03-31) is near the 52-week low of $214.50 and below the high of $324.90.  
- **Returns**: Quarterly return (Q1 2026) = –20.7%; annual return = –0.7%.  
- **Fundamental context**: Price decline occurs despite improved P/E and ROE in latest valuation data. However, *2025-Q4 earnings are not provided*, so the linkage to recent fundamentals is incomplete.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**  
- **Upside**: Valuation reversion; P/E and P/B are at 1-year lows while ROE (32.4%) and net margin (15.7%) are at period highs. If earnings sustain, multiple expansion is plausible.  
- **Downside**: High leverage (3.64× debt-to-equity) and earnings volatility; quarterly EPS has ranged from –$0.36 to $3.11. Missing 2025-Q4 data creates uncertainty about trajectory sustainability. Price has already fallen sharply (-20.7% quarterly) without corresponding visible fundamental deterioration in available data.

**6. Provisional Risk/Reward Conclusion**  
*Quantitative evidence is mixed with significant gaps.* Upside exists from valuation at historical lows paired with recent margin and ROE improvements. Downside risk stems from high leverage, earnings volatility, and missing 2025-Q4 data which may explain the recent price drop. Balance sheet has improved but remains highly geared. Cash flow data absent, limiting quality assessment. **Neutral risk/reward** until 2025-Q4 financials are provided to confirm the sustainability of recent margin expansion and justify the current valuation disconnect.
IBM.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Report: IBM (Decision Date: 2026-04-01)**

**1. Business/Franchise Read (from 10-K, 2026-02-24)**
IBM is a globally integrated technology company focused on enabling client digital transformation through its hybrid cloud platform and artificial intelligence (AI) technology. Its operations are structured into four segments: Software, Consulting, Infrastructure, and Financing. The company differentiates itself by combining deep enterprise incumbency, broad industry and technology expertise, a global partner ecosystem (including hyperscalers like AWS and Microsoft), and a strong research organization. IBM positions itself as a leader in each segment, competing on innovation, breadth of capabilities, and the ability to deliver business value, with a strategic focus on hybrid cloud, AI, and emerging areas like quantum computing.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated from Supplied Text)**
*   **Strategic Partnerships & Product (2026-03-26):** IBM partnered with CrowdStrike and Intel to launch an autonomous AI security hub, integrating CrowdStrike's Charlotte AI with IBM's Autonomous Threat Operations Machine (ATOM) for coordinated threat response.
*   **Corporate Action / M&A (2026-03-20):** IBM was reported to have completed an $11 billion acquisition of Confluent.
*   **Financial Guidance & Market Sentiment (2026-03-02):** Following a Q4 report, IBM forecasted 5% revenue growth for 2025, up from 3% in 2024, with growth expected from hybrid cloud and AI demand. Free cash flow was projected at $13.5 billion for 2025.
*   **Stock Performance & Narrative (2026-03-22, 2026-03-21):** IBM's stock fell approximately 20% from its 52-week high, with investor concern that AI could disrupt its legacy COBOL programming support services. Multiple articles argued this sell-off was overblown, presenting a buying opportunity, citing IBM's history of technological adaptation and its positioning in hybrid cloud and quantum computing.
*   **Competitive & Sector Dynamics (2026-03-23):** Amid a broader market sell-off, computer hardware and data storage companies (a category including IBM) were noted as resilient due to strong AI infrastructure demand.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Legal & Regulatory Exposure.** IBM faces ongoing legal challenges, including a federal court ruling that it broke federal law by forcing age discrimination claims into arbitration (2026-03-26) and a related employee lawsuit (2026-03-09). A historical antitrust reference (2026-03-13) also highlights regulatory scrutiny risk.
    *   *Offset:* The company is actively detailing strategies for navigating intellectual property risks associated with generative AI (2026-03-24), indicating awareness and management of legal landscapes.
*   **Risk: Competitive Pressure & Execution.** IBM operates in highly competitive markets against large hyperscalers (e.g., Google, Amazon, Microsoft) and specialized firms. Its legacy infrastructure business is noted as a potential drag, and overall revenue growth is described as modest (sub-5%) relative to peers (2026-02-10).
    *   *Offset:* The company's strategy leverages its partnerships with these same hyperscalers and focuses on high-value, mission-critical workloads. Its breadth and incumbency are cited as key differentiators.
*   **Risk: Technological Disruption.** Investor concern exists that AI advancements could directly disrupt IBM's traditional service businesses, such as COBOL support (2026-03-22).
    *   *Offset:* The prevailing narrative in the supplied text argues AI is more likely to enhance IBM's service capabilities. The company's investments in its WatsonX AI platform and quantum computing are positioned as future growth catalysts.

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral.** IBM presents a mixed qualitative picture. **Positively**, the company is actively executing on its hybrid cloud and AI strategy through strategic partnerships (CrowdStrike), significant M&A (Confluent), and is guiding for accelerated revenue growth. The market sell-off is framed by some as a potential opportunity given the company's foundational assets. **Negatively**, the franchise faces material legal headwinds, intense competition from larger and more focused players, and investor skepticism about its ability to translate AI trends into growth without cannibalizing legacy revenue streams. The balance between these confirmed positive catalysts and significant, documented risks results in a neutral qualitative stance.

corrections.issue: The quant report states 'Net margin improved from 9.1% (Jan-2026) to 15.7% (Feb-2026)' but the raw valuation history shows net_margin=0.091 on 2026-01-26 and net_margin=0.157 on 2026-02-26. The report's parenthetical dates (Jan-2026, Feb-2026) are imprecise but directionally correct. However, the report does not flag that this jump is likely an artifact of the 2025-Q4 earnings being incorporated into the trailing calculation (the 10-K was filed 2026-02-24), not a genuine margin expansion in the Jan-Feb period. The report should note this mechanical effect.
corrections.required_action: Add a note clarifying that the net margin and ROE jumps at 2026-02-26 are likely due to the 2025-Q4 annual results being incorporated into trailing metrics upon 10-K filing, not an organic intra-quarter improvement.

corrections.issue: The qual report states 'IBM was reported to have completed an $11 billion acquisition of Confluent' citing 2026-03-20. The raw evidence (Deal Dispatch article) mentions 'IBM's $11B acquisition of Confluent' in a summary of M&A activity. However, Confluent (CFLT) is a publicly traded company with a market cap well below $11B as of recent periods, and the raw evidence does not provide deal terms, confirmation status, or whether this was completed vs. announced. The report should note the source is a news summary and the status/confirmation is not independently verified in the supplied evidence.
corrections.required_action: Qualify the Confluent acquisition claim as sourced from a single news summary; note that deal completion, terms, and target identity are not independently confirmed in the supplied evidence.

corrections.issue: The quant report's 'Financial Trajectory' section states 'Quarterly revenue fluctuates between $14.46B (2024-Q1) and $17.55B (2024-Q4). The latest reported quarter (2025-Q2) is $16.98B.' This is accurate per the raw data. However, the report also states 'Latest EPS (2025-Q2) is $2.31' and '2025-Q4 data unavailable' — yet the raw evidence includes a 2025-12-31 balance sheet entry (total_assets=151.88B) from a filing dated 2026-02-24 with period_end=2025-12-31. The 10-K was filed, so 2025-Q4 income data exists in the real world but is absent from the supplied income statement pack. The report correctly flags this gap but should be more explicit that the 10-K filing date confirms Q4 results were published, yet the income statement data was not supplied in the evidence pack.
corrections.required_action: Clarify that the 2025-Q4 income data gap is a data-supply issue (the 10-K was filed 2026-02-24 per the evidence) rather than the data not yet existing.

unsupported_claims: : The qual report's framing of the Confluent acquisition as 'completed' (2026-03-20) is not fully supported — the raw evidence is a single news summary listing it among several M&A deals, without the deal status or terms being independently confirmed in the supplied packs.

: The quant report's statement 'Net margin improved from 9.1% (Jan-2026) to 15.7% (Feb-2026)' implies a genuine operational improvement over one month; the raw evidence shows this is more likely a mechanical recalculation when 2025-Q4 annual results were incorporated into trailing metrics around the 10-K filing date (2026-02-24). The report does not make this distinction.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: IBM's stock has fallen ~20% from its 52-week high, pushing its P/E to 21.7x — the bottom of its 1-year range — while improving its earnings yield to 4.6%. However, the sharp decline likely reflects a genuine repricing of AI-disruption risk to legacy services, high leverage (3.64x debt-to-equity), and missing 2025-Q4 income data that prevents confirmation of the recent margin/ROE improvement as sustainable. The risk/reward is mildly positive but conviction is limited by data gaps and unresolved competitive headwinds.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: IBM's stock has fallen ~20% from its 52-week high, pushing its P/E to 21.7x — the bottom of its 1-year range — while improving its earnings yield to 4.6%. However, the sharp decline likely reflects a genuine repricing of AI-disruption risk to legacy services, high leverage (3.64x debt-to-equity), and missing 2025-Q4 income data that prevents confirmation of the recent margin/ROE improvement as sustainable. The risk/reward is mildly positive but conviction is limited by data gaps and unresolved competitive headwinds.
key_factors: Valuation at 1-year lows: P/E of 21.7x (vs. 1-year range 20.5–51.2), P/B of 7.0x, and earnings yield of 4.6% provide a margin of safety after a -20.7% quarterly decline, with price near the 52-week low of $214.50.

Margin and ROE improvement may be mechanical: Net margin jumped from 9.1% to 15.7% and ROE from 22.0% to 32.4% around the 10-K filing (2026-02-24), likely reflecting incorporation of 2025-Q4 annual results into trailing metrics rather than organic operational improvement — sustainability unconfirmed without income statement detail.

AI-disruption narrative weighing on sentiment: Investor concern that AI could cannibalize IBM's legacy COBOL and services revenue drove the sell-off, though IBM's hybrid cloud/AI strategy (WatsonX, $13.5B guided FCF for 2025, Confluent acquisition) and partnerships (CrowdStrike, Intel) provide qualitative offsets.

High leverage and earnings volatility persist: Debt-to-equity of 3.64x remains elevated, and quarterly EPS has ranged from -$0.36 to $3.11, creating downside risk if the 2025-Q4 results disappoint or the margin improvement proves non-recurring.

et their expectations.
Further, we do not view the interconnected experience as a specific transaction; rather, we believe it encompasses an entire journey from inspiration and know-how, to purchase and fulfillment, to post-purchase support. Customers continue to expect more personalized messaging, and we remain focused on connecting marketing activities with the online and in-store experiences to create seamless engagement across channels. From the inspirational point of the purchase journey to providing product know-how, we continue to invest in the infrastructure and capabilities needed to deliver the most relevant marketing messages to our customers based on what is important to them today.
Store Experience
Our stores remain the hub of our business, and we continue to invest to improve the customer shopping experience through easier navigation and increased convenience and speed of checkout. In fiscal 2025, we continued to leverage the investments made in our store experience over the past several years to operate effectively and meet customer expectations, as well as investing in dedicated teams to better meet the needs of our Pros in-store. We have also empowered our customers with additional self-help tools, including mobile application-enabled store navigation and Magic Apron. Our Home Depot mobile application provides store-specific maps that allow customers to pinpoint the exact location of an item on their mobile devices. We also recognize that another critical part of delivering an excellent customer experience in the store is having products on our shelves, and we have enhanced our use of technology such as Computer Vision, which provides greater visibility into where product is located, including both on shelves and in the overhead space, enabling us to drive higher on-shelf availability. We believe these investments are driving higher customer satisfaction scores, and we will continue to invest to improve the customer experience. In addition, we have identified areas that have experienced significant population growth or where market voids exist. In fiscal 2023, we initiated a plan to open approximately 80 new stores over a five-year period to address those opportunities, and in fiscal 2025, we opened 12 new stores. We expect to complete this goal of approximately 80 new stores in fiscal 2027, after which point we plan to open approximately 15 to 20 new stores per year. These new stores will continue to help relieve pressure at existing high-volume stores and add capacity in areas with less store coverage, helping us to improve the customer experience and drive sales growth.
Investing in the Associate Experience.
We continually strive to improve our store operations by removing complexity and inefficient processes, allowing our associates to spend more of their time serving our customers. To this end, we have continued to focus on process improvements like optimizing product flow to improve on-shelf availability and reduce the amount of time store associates spend locating products, as well as expanding in-aisle, real-time mobile learning tools for our associates' development and assistance with customer questions. Our associates are also using web-enabled handheld devices to help them more efficiently meet the needs of the business and serve customers. These digital "hdPhone" devices offer enhanced functionality that allows associates to readily query inventory, access applications that support customer service, and assist with locating products. Our
Fiscal 2025 Form 10-K 5
Table of Contents
hdPhones also give our U.S. Home Depot store associates access to Sidekick, an application that directs associates to bays where product is low or out of stock and helps our associates prioritize the highest value tasks more effectively. In addition, we are using labor model tools to better align associate activity with customer needs. This includes transitioning more of our store tasking to our merchandising execution team (MET), leveraging MET associates' expertise to reinforce in-aisle associates' focus on excellent customer service.
Investing in Safety.
We remain committed to maintaining a safe shopping and working environment for our customers and associates. We accomplish this by creating a strong culture of safety, building on our core value of Taking Care of Our People, that starts from the top with engaged leaders who empower associates to make decisions that prioritize the safety of everyone. We use data to identify areas of greatest risk, including emerging risks, and invest in tools, equipment and technology to reduce those risks in our packaging, processes, and behaviors. Our associate training and awareness initiatives target individual roles and responsibilities, integrating with overall strategies that promote physical and psychological safety and emotional wellness.
We empower trained EH&S associates to continuously evaluate, develop, implement and enforce policies, processes and programs in our stores, facilities and offices across the Company. Our EH&S policies are woven into our everyday operations for site, district and regional teams, and integrate with operating platforms to provide safety line-of-sight to associates and their leaders. Common program elements include daily store inspection checklists; routine follow-up audits from our store-based safety team members; preventative maintenance programs to promote equipment and physical space safety; and departmental merchandising safety standards.
OUR SUPPLY CHAIN
We continue to focus on building best-in-class competitive advantages in our supply chain to be responsive to our customers' expectations for how, when and where they choose to receive our products and services. As part of enhancing the interconnected experience, over the past several years, we have invested in our supply chain network with the goal of achieving the fastest, most efficient and most reliable delivery capabilities in home improvement. With that investment now largely complete, our supply chain initiatives have positioned us to operate effectively and meet our customers' needs for product availability and fast, reliable delivery.
We centrally forecast and replenish the vast majority of our Home Depot store products through sophisticated inventory management systems and utilize our network of distribution centers to serve both our stores' and customers' needs. Our supply chain includes multiple distribution center platforms in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico that are tailored to meet the needs of our stores and customers based on types of products, location, transportation, and delivery requirements. These include rapid deployment centers, stocking distribution centers, bulk distribution centers, FDCs, and direct fulfillment centers, among others. Over the past several years, we have invested to further automate and mechanize our rapid deployment center network to drive greater efficiency and faster movement of product.
We have also enhanced our supply chain network with our expanded fulfillment facilities designed to drive speed and reliability of delivery for our customers. Our 'ship from best location' initiative seeks to optimize delivery speed, reliability, cost, and capacity by leveraging machine learning algorithms to route orders to the optimal fulfillment node. In many markets, we offer same day or next day delivery of a multitude of products through our stores and fulfillment centers. We have market delivery operations that function as local hubs to consolidate freight for dispatch to customers for the final mile of delivery, with a focus on appliances. Because we typically do not stock appliances in our facilities, we have invested in our capabilities to control more of our appliance delivery end-to-end, manage our appliance delivery volume through our market delivery operations, and expand our last mile delivery capacity.
